questions.question: Which is an example of a generative AI output?
options: A model predicting whether an email is spam or not spam
A system generating a new image from a text prompt
A model counting how many rows are in a database table
A program sorting numbers from smallest to largest

questions.question: Why might a generative model sometimes reproduce patterns it has seen before?
options: Because it is trained on examples and can mimic repeated content
Because it always copies and pastes its dataset
Because it cannot learn statistical relationships
Because it only works on very small datasets

questions.question: What are guardrails used for in generative AI applications?
options: To make the model forget training data
To constrain outputs and reduce errors or unsafe content
To guarantee the model never makes mistakes
To replace the need for any verification

questions.question: Which combination best matches the lesson’s recommended approach for many applications?
options: Use vague prompts and avoid verification
Use clear prompts, restricted sources, verification, and human oversight
Avoid using any constraints so creativity is higher
Rely only on the model output without checking
